L1.area: File download, caching, and archive extraction utilities

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where malicious tar archives can bypass path filtering checks during extraction. The extraction logic does not apply sufficient security filters to prevent symlink-based attacks that exploit path length limits, allowing files to be written outside the intended extraction directory.

L3.area: Archive extraction and path filtering logic within file utility and model saving modules
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where malicious tar archives can bypass path filtering checks during extraction. A symlink chain with excessively long paths can cause resolution failures during extraction, falling back to literal path interpretation and allowing directory traversal. The extraction call lacks Python's built-in data filter that would block unsafe symlinks and paths.

ground_truth.reason: The extract_archive function in file_utils.py uses tarfile.extractall() without the filter="data" parameter, allowing malicious tar archives with PATH_MAX symlink chains to bypass path filtering and write files outside the intended extraction directory.
